sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see:
- Blocks are at rows 25-29, cols 19-23 (on the horizontal bar)
- White cross is at rows 31-33, cols 21-22 (below the blocks in the left gray area)
- The white cross reappeared when blocks moved UP
So the white cross is still there, just below the blocks. The blocks are on the horizontal bar (rows 25-29), and the white cross is in the left gray area (rows 31-33).
Now I need to figure out the goal. Looking at the top area, there's a blue L-shape pattern inside a black frame. This might be the target pattern that the blocks need to match.
Looking at the passage to the top:
- Rows 17-24, cols 37-41: ##### (gray passage going up)
- This leads to the black frame at rows 8-16, cols 32-40
The blocks need to get to the top area. From the current position (rows 25-29, cols 19-23), I need to:
1. Move RIGHT to align with the passage at cols 37-41
2. Move UP through the passage to reach the top area
Currently blocks are at cols 19-23. The passage is at cols 37-41. Each RIGHT moves 5 columns.
- RIGHT 1: cols 24-28
- RIGHT 2: cols 29-33
- RIGHT 3: cols 34-38
- RIGHT 4: cols 39-43
RIGHT 3 would put blocks at cols 34-38, which overlaps with the passage at cols 37-38. But the passage is only 5 columns wide (cols 37-41), and the blocks are 4 columns wide. So RIGHT 3 should work.
Actually wait, let me reconsider. The horizontal bar is at rows 25-29, and it extends from cols 14 to about col 53. The passage is at cols 37-41. So I can move RIGHT along the horizontal bar.
Let me try RIGHT 3 to move to cols 34-38, then UP to go through the passage.

</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
